source_record.title: Lipid metabolism in multiple myeloma: pathogenesis, therapeutic opportunities, and future directions.
source_record.study_context: Frontiers in Oncology review discussing lipid metabolism in multiple myeloma, including lipid processing, fatty-acid uptake, bone marrow adipocyte interaction, tumor microenvironment effects, and drug-resistance context. This public extraction uses the review only as second-source pathway-state navigation context.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source describes lipid metabolism as linked to multiple myeloma cell survival, proliferation, tumor microenvironment interaction, and drug-resistance context, including altered lipogenesis, lipid uptake, lipid droplet formation, lipid trafficking, and fatty-acid uptake from bone marrow adipocyte context.
limitations: Review-level source; not a cohort-level MRD residual clone measurement.
Does not second-source the exact Cui 2024 posttreatment single-cell fatty-acid oxidation signal.
Supports metabolic branch navigation only, not treatment selection, supplement guidance, prognosis, biomarker use, or clinical actionability.
Requires primary-source extraction before any claim upgrade.

what_this_supports: The metabolic-resistance-state-v0 branch can move to covered-for-v0-navigation as public artifact coverage because it now has one single-cell study signal and one review-level lipid-metabolism context source.
The geometry should preserve metabolism as a residual biological state branch while keeping pathway context descriptive.
Metabolic context should stay linked to source type, assay or review level, tumor microenvironment context, and uncertainty.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not recommend metabolic interventions, supplements, imaging, biomarkers, drug targets, or treatment strategies.
It does not establish that a metabolic resistance state is dominant across multiple myeloma settings.
It does not interpret any patient MRD result or residual disease state.
It does not validate clinical actionability or cure claims.

review_notes: Created for Phase 4 productive-movement testing in mrd-resistance-geometry-proof-plan-v0.
Source details checked against the publisher page and PubMed-indexed metadata visible through search results.
The extraction intentionally weakens actionability language and uses the source as review-level navigation context only.
